title: Universal Front Door

content.statement: A business grows faster when it has one clear, high-value entry offer that attracts the right people into the ecosystem.
content.question: What is the one obvious first step people should take with your business?
content.equation: Customer Entry = Clear Offer × Low Friction × High Perceived Value
content.mental_model: Welcoming front door, everyone knows where to enter.
content.business_lens: Create one obvious first step that leads customers into the larger business model.
content.life_lens: Make it easy for the right people to understand how to work with you, support you, or join your world.
sop: Identify your ideal customer's easiest first yes
Make the entry offer valuable and low-friction
Connect the entry offer to your deeper paid offers
Track conversion from entry to next step
Improve the front door before adding more entrances
content.explainer: The Universal Front Door is the main way people enter your business. It could be a free trial, a low-cost offer, a diagnostic call, a waitlist, a template, a sample, or a simple starter product. Mailchimp’s free first contacts offer is an example of a front door that made the product easy to try.
questions: What is the one obvious first step people should take with your business?
If a stranger found you today, what's the easiest yes you could offer them in the next five minutes?
Which of your current offers are actually competing with each other for that first-step spot?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Clear Offer 8, Low Friction 9, High Perceived Value 6. Score = 8 times 9 times 6 = 432 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means strangers convert into your ecosystem almost automatically. Lower means people bounce before they ever experience your value.

caption:
Principle Nº 251: Universal Front Door

A business grows faster when it has one clear, high-value entry offer that attracts the right people into the ecosystem.

Ask yourself: What is the one obvious first step people should take with your business?
